zh.lifeSummary: 當Rhys ap Philip於大約1240年出生在威爾士卡馬特史爾費時，他的父親Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym31歲，母親Elen ferch Trahaearn22歲。他和Jonet ferch Llywelyn至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rhys ap Philip于大约1240年出生在威尔士卡馬特史爾費时，他的父亲Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym31岁，母亲Elen ferch Trahaearn22岁。他和Jonet ferch Llywelyn至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Varughese, Mathai, Babu, Reji, Anil, Binu, Neena, Prema, Raju, Reena, Susheela, Aleykutty.

Scottish, English, German, Dutch, and Jewish: from the Greek personal name Philippos (from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’). In the New Testament this name is borne by one of the apostles; it was also borne by various other early Christian saints. It owes part of its popularity to the medieval romances about Alexander the Great, whose father was Philip of Macedon. As a Jewish name, it represents a borrowing of the personal name from Christians. As a Highland Scottish surname, it represents an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Spanish Felipe , Catalan Felip, Polish, Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or Croatian Filip , Albanian Filipi , and their derivatives (see examples at Philips ). The name Philip is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Philipose and Pothen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Phillip .
French (southern) and Breton (Finistère): variant of   Philippe  .

variants.name: McKillip
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhilib ‘son of Philip’ (see Philip ). See also McPhillips .

variants.name: Felipe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Juana, Miguel, Orlando, Pedro, Angel, Carlos, Emilio, Jesus.
Spanish: from the personal name Felipe, an equivalent of Philippe .

variants.name: Lips
meanings: North German, Flemish, and Dutch: from a shortened form of the personal name Philips, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Lipps
meanings: North German: from a shortened form of the personal name Philipps, from Latin Philippus (see Philip ).

variants.name: Pepin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Camille, Emile, Pierre, Gilles, Lucien, Normand, Alcide, Fernand, Jacques, Marcel.

French (Pépin) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Pipin, of uncertain, probably onomatopoeic origin. The name was borne by several Frankish kings, most notably Pepin le Bref, father of Charlemagne, and remained popular throughout the early Middle Ages.
English: from the unrecorded Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal names Pipin, Pepin, Pipun, pet forms of Philip . Compare Pipe .

variants.name: Lippens
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alfons, Alphons.  French Andre.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from a shortened form of the personal name   Philip  .

variants.name: Philbin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Philbín, ‘son of Philbin (Irish Philbín or Filibín)’, a pet form of Philip .

variants.name: Lipman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Mayer, Chaim, Dov, Isadore, Jakob, Meyer, Pinchas.

Dutch: habitational name from a farm named Lipman or perhaps Lippink, referring to a certain Lippo and his kin. Lippo is a pet form of an ancient Germanic personal name composed with the element leiba ‘offspring, son’, or of the personal name Philip .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Lipman, derived from Middle High German words liep ‘dear, beloved’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Philipose
meanings: Indian (Kerala): from the personal name Philipose, derived from Greek Philippos (see Philip ), found among Christians in southern India. — Note: Since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, this name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Philibert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Fernand, Mirielle, Pascale, Pierrot.

French: from the personal name Philibert, from ancient Germanic Filuberht, composed of the elements fila ‘much, very’ + berht ‘bright’, which was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, abbot of Jumièges. The spelling has been influenced by personal names of Greek origin based on philein ‘to love’, e.g. Philip .

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Rhys ap Philip was born about 1240, in Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Wales, his father, Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym, was 31 and his mother, Elen ferch Trahaearn, was 22. He had at least 1 son with Jonet ferch Llywelyn.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rhys ap Philip est né environ 1240, à Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Galles, son père, Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elen ferch Trahaearn, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Jonet ferch Llywelyn.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rhys ap Philip um 1240 in Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym 31 und seine Mutter Elen ferch Trahaearn 22. Er hatte mit Jonet ferch Llywelyn mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rhys ap Philip è nato il circa 1240 nel Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Galles, il suo padre, Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Elen ferch Trahaearn, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Jonet ferch Llywelyn.

ja.Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Wales: ウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアグウインフ
ja.lifeSummary: Rhys ap Philipが約1240年にウェールズカーマースエンシュアイアグウインフで生まれた時，父Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilymは31歳，母Elen ferch Trahaearnは22歳であった。彼はJonet ferch Llywelynとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Rhys ap Philip(이)가 대략 1240년 웨일스 카마테실르 그w이프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Elen ferch Trahaearn(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Jonet ferch Llywelyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rhys ap Philip nasceu aproximadamente 1240, em Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Gales, seu pai, Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elen ferch Trahaearn, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Jonet ferch Llywelyn.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rhys ap Philip родился около 1240 г. в местности гвинф, кармартеншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elen ferch Trahaearn, 22. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Jonet ferch Llywelyn.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rhys ap Philip nació aproximadamente 1240, en Gwynfe, Carmarthenshire, Gales, su papá, Philip " Fychan" ap Gwilym, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elen ferch Trahaearn, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Jonet ferch Llywelyn.
